title: Age as a superpower (I'm not "old school", I'm "whole school")
body:
I was born in 1968 and I've seen the Internet being born from within.

I coded when browsers were a jungle, I've adapted to dozens of frameworks that have come and gone, and I've learned that the most important thing is not the latest "shiny tool", but the ability to solve problems.

Now, however, the market seems to be looking for more of a "22-year-old senior with 10 years of experience" than a professional who has lived through all the battles. Ageism in the IT sector is a strategic error:

-   **Experience:** I've seen what works and what doesn't.
-   **Adaptability:** If I've survived the evolution of the web, I can learn anything new in an afternoon.
-   **Temperament:** I don't panic when the server goes down or the deploy fails.

Talent has no expiration date. Who's in for valuing real seniority?
